questions.statement: In the following question, four number pairs are given. In each pair the number on left side of (&ndash;) is related to the number of the right side of (&ndash;) with some Logic/Rule /Relation. Three pairs are similar on basis of same Logic/Rule/Relation. Select the odd one out from the given alternatives. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g.13 &ndash; Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed)
In the following question, select the odd letter/letters from the given alternatives.
In a certain code language, &#39;ANMGRS&#39; is coded as &#39;PODJKX&#39;. What is the code for &#39;CHAIRS&#39; in that code language?

<p>Select the correct mirror image of the given combination when the mirror is placed at XY as shown.<br />
<img alt="" src="//storage.googleapis.com/tb-img/production/22/09/F1_Savita_SSC_26-9-22_D21.png" style="width: 115px; height: 94px;" /></p>
Z and Y are daughters of Q. Y is married to P. P has two sons, L and M. X is the husband of Q. How is X related to M?

<p>Select the figure from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series.</p>

<p><img alt="" src="//storage.googleapis.com/tb-img/production/24/02/F2_Savita_SSC_20-2-24_D1_V2.png" style="width: 397px; height: 82px;" /></p>

<p>The given Venn diagram shows the number of children in a complex who play a particular&nbsp;sport. How many children play Cricket as well as Chess?</p>

<p><img alt="" src="//storage.googleapis.com/tb-img/production/21/12/F1%20_Killi_16-12-21_Savita_D24.png" style="width: 285px; height: 126px;" /></p>
